rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the ground truth's actual substantive concern (dsocek's suggestion to derive LoRA naming from `_packed_modules_mapping`/`stacked_params_mapping` for fused-projection renames), so recall is low for both. Both instead converge on the same well-grounded, cross-validated point (deriving `default_components` from `_dit_modules` instead of a fourth hardcoded list), with concrete file/line evidence and actionable suggestions. X adds a distinct, valuable catch (text-encoder LoRA silently no-oping, same bug class as the PR's stated problem) while Y adds a distinct but more generic point (missing SDXL integration test) plus more rigorous grep-evidenced verification, though at the cost of restating the same duplication finding twice.
